Overview

Address
1155 STERLINGTON HIGHWAY, FARMERVILLE, LA 71241
Phone

150

94 (63% of beds filled)

Provider type

Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.58 3.47 3.99 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.27 0.61 0.30 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.82 0.77 0.92 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.48 2.09 2.77 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.09 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.11
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.46
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
38.3%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.23
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.89
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-10-01 8 8 0 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-31 12 9 3 64 1 64
Total weighted health score
58.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (24)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2025-10-01 2025-11-18
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. E 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-31 2024-12-06
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-11-15 2023-12-24
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2023-11-15 2023-12-24
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-11-15 2023-12-24
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-11-15 2023-12-24
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-06-07 2023-07-15
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-06-07 2023-07-15
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-06-07 2023-07-15
